compiled_state.title: .test-index: Agent uses wrong RED marker syntax (missing space) -> record-test-status.sh reports file not found
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
During a worktree session fixing MERGE_HEAD deadlock, the agent attempted to add RED markers to .test-index using an incorrect format: `tests/foo.sh[test_name]` (no space before bracket). The correct format requires a space: `tests/foo.sh [test_name]`.

## Observed Behavior
Agent wrote: `tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-part4.sh[test_merge_commit_filters_incoming_only]`
record-test-status.sh reported: WARNING: .test-index entry points to nonexistent file: tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-part4.sh[test_merge_commit_filters_incoming_only]
Agent required a second attempt after diagnosing the format mismatch from source code.

## Expected Behavior
Agent should use correct format on first attempt. A minimal usage example in CLAUDE.md or .test-index header comments would prevent this.

## Resolution
Add a one-line usage example to the .test-index format documentation (CLAUDE.md or record-test-status.sh header). Keep it minimal — one example line is sufficient.

comments.body: Investigation (INTERMEDIATE, opus): No malformed RED markers found in .test-index (only 1 RED marker on line 260, properly formatted with space). Parser requires whitespace before bracket. Ticket describes an authoring hazard but no current violation. ESCALATE: user decision needed on (a) close as no-repro, (b) relax parser regex, or (c) add pre-commit validator.
comments.author: Test
